sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Trading the Move, Not the Narrative: (NJR) Edition
feed.summary: Stock Traders Daily provides an AI-driven analysis for Newjersey Resources Corporation (NJR), recommending a wait-and-see approach due to neutral sentiment across all time horizons despite an ongoing breakout. The analysis offers three distinct trading strategies—Position, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging—with specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels, and highlights support and resistance signals across near-term, mid-term, and long-term timeframes.

feed.title: PyroGenesis secures new contract for green cement development
feed.summary: PyroGenesis Canada Inc. has secured a C$450,000 contract with a US structural materials entity for green cement development. This contract marks PyroGenesis' entry into the cement industry, a sector responsible for over 8% of global CO2 emissions. The company will design and build a system for lab-scale cement production using plasma torch technology to minimize emissions, aligning with its goal of helping the industry decarbonize.

feed.title: Should MACOM’s New PCIe 7.0 and Optical Lineup at OFC 2026 Reshape MTSI’s AI Narrative?
feed.summary: MACOM Technology Solutions showcased PCIe 7.0 optimized linear equalizer and cross-point switch devices and an extensive range of advanced photonics and interconnect solutions at OFC 2026, underlining its ambition to be a key enabler of next-generation AI-scale data centers. This move aims to deepen MACOM's exposure to higher-speed PCIe and 1.6T optical ecosystems, reinforcing its AI infrastructure narrative. However, risks remain regarding volatile orders and execution on fab utilization and cost improvements, which could impact margins despite the positive product announcements.

feed.title: Tapestry Manager, Procurement - New York, New York, United States job with Tapestry | 306977
feed.summary: Tapestry is seeking a Manager, Marketing Procurement to join its Global Procurement & Category Management team in New York, New York. This role involves leading strategic sourcing initiatives across various marketing categories for Tapestry's brands, including Coach and Kate Spade New York. The successful candidate will manage a global advertising and marketing indirect procurement category exceeding $650MM+, focusing on value creation, cost containment, and vendor contract management.

feed.title: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) Downgraded to "Hold" Rating by Wall Street Zen
feed.summary: Wall Street Zen downgraded Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) to a "hold" rating, though the stock still holds a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $89.55 from other analysts who have increased their price targets. The downgrade follows Cisco's beat-and-raise Q2 earnings, but concerns linger over potential margin compression due to higher memory costs and a hardware-heavy product mix, along with recent insider selling. Despite positive news like increased AI orders and product share gains, these risks and insider activity may impact near-term sentiment.

feed.title: United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) Downgraded by Wall Street Zen to "Hold"
feed.summary: United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) has been downgraded by Wall Street Zen from a "buy" to a "hold" rating, despite 15 other analysts maintaining a "buy" rating and a consensus price target of $133.86. The downgrade reflects concerns over heightened oil prices and geopolitical risks impacting margins, even though United reported an EPS beat and strong FY2026 guidance. Company insiders and institutional investors have also recently adjusted their holdings, contributing to market volatility.

feed.title: Bullish Analyst Commentary on Rates and Deals Might Change The Case For Investing In Bank of New York Mellon (BK)
feed.summary: Billionaire investor Mario Gabelli and other analysts are bullish on Bank of New York Mellon (BK), citing its exposure to a steeper interest-rate curve, strong deal activity, and supportive equity markets as key tailwinds. They also highlighted BNY's capital-light model and sticky client relationships as foundational strengths. While these factors reinforce reasons for investing in BNY, the article cautions that its reliance on favorable market conditions means sustained volatility or weaker equity markets could pose a significant risk.

feed.title: Assessing Travelers (TRV) Valuation After Recent Share Price Strength And Long Term Returns

While analysts have a consensus price target slightly below its current trading price of $302.49, a "most popular narrative" suggests TRV is 20% undervalued with a fair value of $303.23. The article highlights that the valuation is based on assumptions about future earnings and margins, but notes that risks like catastrophe losses or squeezing margins could quickly change the outlook.

feed.title: Is Truist’s Plaid Partnership Quietly Redefining Its Digital Efficiency Story for Investors (TFC)?

feed.title: Lawsuit Alert: Investors who lost money with shares of PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) should contact the Shareholders Foundation
feed.summary: A lawsuit has been filed against PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) on behalf of investors who allege securities law violations due to false and misleading statements regarding the company's financial targets and growth projections. The lawsuit claims PayPal issued overwhelmingly positive statements while concealing issues with its salesforce and being overly optimistic about customer adoption. Investors who purchased shares between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026, are encouraged to contact the Shareholders Foundation before the April 20, 2026 deadline.

feed.title: Why Jack Henry & Associates Just Hit Every US Bank’s Radar
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates is a pivotal financial technology provider for thousands of US community banks and credit unions, offering core banking systems, digital banking, payments, and fraud tools. While not a flashy consumer-facing brand, its technology underpins much of the American banking experience, making it a critical player in the evolving fintech landscape. The article emphasizes its role as the "picks-and-shovels" of digital banking, highlighting its stable, recurring revenue model and increasing relevance amid shifts towards cloud and API-first banking.

feed.title: Analyst Optimism Surges for Dow Inc. as Supply Dynamics Shift
feed.summary: Analyst sentiment for Dow Inc. has significantly improved, with major Wall Street institutions raising their outlook and price targets for the chemicals giant. This optimism is driven by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which are expected to tighten global polyethylene supply and favor North American producers like Dow due to their advantageous cost structures. Dow's stock has already seen a positive response, gaining over 54% year-to-date and approaching its 52-week high.

feed.title: Amazon's Ad-Free Prime Video Plan Will Now Cost $2 More In US— Here's What To Know
feed.summary: Amazon is increasing the price of its ad-free Prime Video plan by $2, rebranding it as "Prime Video Ultra" and adding features like 4K streaming and more simultaneous device support. This move follows a trend of price hikes across several major streaming services including Netflix, Disney+, and Paramount+. Despite these price adjustments, Amazon reports continued growth in Prime Video's audience, with over 315 million global ad-supported viewers.

feed.title: Qualcomm SVP Grech sells $77,563 in QCOM stock
feed.summary: Patricia Y. Grech, SVP, Chief Accounting Officer at Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM), sold 581 shares of common stock for $77,563 on March 12, 2026, executed under a pre-arranged trading plan. The sale occurred while QCOM shares were near their 52-week low and down 24% year-to-date, despite InvestingPro suggesting the stock is undervalued. This transaction follows mixed Q4 2026 results and various analyst ratings, with the company facing challenges like memory shortages, potential business loss from Apple, and high activist attractiveness.

feed.title: KeyCorp Stock (ISIN: US4932671088) Shows Stability Amid Volatility, Attracting DACH Yield Hunters
feed.summary: KeyCorp stock (ISIN: US4932671088) is presented as a stable investment opportunity for European investors, particularly those in the DACH region, due to its solid fundamentals, attractive 3.97% dividend yield, and low-risk rating. The article highlights the regional bank's resilience in a volatile market, its robust capital buffers, and its appeal for income-focused portfolios despite uncertainties in US interest rates and pressures on regional banks. KeyCorp's consistent dividend policy, strong credit quality, and moderate valuation compared to peers are emphasized as key attractions for investors seeking diversification and reliable yields.

feed.title: United Airlines Holdings (UAL) Is Down 5.9% After Jet Fuel Spike Squeezes Margins Amid Record Sales
feed.summary: United Airlines Holdings (UAL) saw its stock drop by 5.9% following a surge in jet fuel prices due to Middle East tensions, impacting margins despite record 2025 Q4 revenue of US$15.40 billion and strong demand. The company is pursuing long-term strategies, including fleet expansion and upgraded premium offerings, to offset these cost pressures and leverage higher-yielding revenue streams. However, the unexpected fuel spike adds risk to its highly leveraged balance sheet and previously optimistic investment outlook.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Xcel Energy (XEL) Could Change Following $800 Million Junior Subordinated Notes Offering
feed.summary: Xcel Energy recently issued $800 million in 5.75% Fixed-to-Fixed Reset Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056, a move to finance its ambitious $60 billion infrastructure program. While these notes offer financial flexibility and support projects like their targeted 6 GW contracted data center capacity, they also highlight the challenge of funding long-term assets without excessively impacting interest coverage or causing shareholder dilution. The issuance is a key part of Xcel Energy's strategy to execute large-load growth but requires careful consideration of rising interest costs and ongoing funding needs.

feed.title: Equinix (EQIX) Valuation In Focus As New CFO Olivier Leonetti Prepares To Succeed Keith Taylor
feed.summary: Equinix (EQIX) has appointed Olivier Leonetti as its new CFO, succeeding Keith Taylor. The company's stock has seen significant returns, and while Simply Wall St's narrative suggests a 5.6% undervaluation with a fair value of $1,027.15 per share, its P/E ratio of 70.6x suggests a potential valuation risk compared to industry averages. Investors are encouraged to consider both the growth prospects from its AI infrastructure investments and potential risks to its valuation.

feed.title: Illinois Tool Works Stock Falls Amid Mixed Analyst Views and Strong FY2026 Guidance
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) shares fell 2.2% despite the company reporting stronger-than-expected Q4 results with $2.72 EPS and raising its FY2026 EPS guidance to $11.00-$11.40, significantly above consensus. Analysts maintain a "Reduce" consensus rating with a target of $276, reflecting mixed sentiment regarding valuation premiums amid strong fundamentals and its diversified industrial model providing resilience. The company's robust margins, strong cash flow, and dividend aristocrat status are highlighted.

feed.title: Burke & Herbert Advances LINKBANCORP Merger With Board Refresh
feed.summary: Burke & Herbert Bank & Company is advancing its merger with LINKBANCORP by appointing two LINKBANCORP directors, Diane Poillon and Kristen Snyder, to its post-merger board, increasing the board size. This move is part of a planned governance refresh, which also includes the departure of several existing directors due to retirement or age limits. The strategic appointments aim to ensure governance stability and independence during the integration, with the company's stock currently holding a "Hold" rating from analysts and an "Outperform" assessment by TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark.

The report will determine if the company can meet high analyst expectations, with the stock's recent surge indicating considerable optimism already priced in.

feed.title: Will Kimbell’s US$100 Million Buyback Shift Kimbell Royalty Partners' (KRP) Capital Return Narrative?
feed.summary: Kimbell Royalty Partners (KRP) has authorized a US$100 million unit repurchase program, aiming to enhance capital return to unitholders and provide flexibility in its capital structure. This buyback complements the existing distribution policy, allowing management to balance payouts and debt reduction amidst commodity price volatility. While the buyback supports the short-term capital return story, the core investment narrative still hinges on drilling activity, acquisition returns, and managing competition for mineral interests.

feed.title: Regions Financial Stock: Hidden Dividend Play or Risk Trap for 2026?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Regions Financial (RF), a US regional bank, questioning whether it is a hidden dividend play or a risk trap amidst AI hype on Wall Street. It explores the stock's attractiveness due to its dividend yield, interest rate sensitivity, and ongoing regional bank risks. The piece provides an overview for potential investors, highlighting both the bull and bear cases for the stock in the context of anticipated rate cuts and credit quality concerns for 2026.

feed.title: Does NXP (NXPI) Pairing Higher Dividend With New Edge AI Chip Hint At A Strategic Shift?
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors recently announced an interim cash dividend of US$1.014 per share for Q1 2026 and unveiled a new i.MX 93W applications processor with integrated AI acceleration. This dual announcement highlights NXP's strategy of combining strong capital returns with product innovation, particularly targeting the automotive and industrial IoT sectors. The article suggests this move could simplify customer designs, reduce system-level costs, and potentially influence NXP's investment narrative positively amidst ongoing analyst scrutiny.

feed.title: Moderna Impfstoff (Spikevax) in 2026: Key Updates
feed.summary: The article provides an update on Moderna's Spikevax (Moderna Impfstoff) in 2026, highlighting its continued role in COVID-19 vaccination efforts due to its established efficacy, safety record confirmed by post-marketing surveillance, and adaptability against evolving variants. It discusses the vaccine's benefits, such as logistical handling, broad approval spectrum across age groups, and its positive impact on Moderna Inc. stock performance. Experts affirm Spikevax as a vital tool in immunization strategies, praising its precision and cost-effectiveness.

feed.title: Unlocking Better Construction Outcomes with Data
feed.summary: The article discusses how construction companies can leverage data to improve outcomes. It highlights that while a lot of data is collected, much of it goes unused due to lack of standardization and structured approaches. The key is not just to digitize workflows but to implement a strategy for effective data utilization, as demonstrated by companies like APTIM, J.E. Dunn, and Barton Mallow, with a focus on building the right teams to develop these strategies.

feed.title: Does KLA (KLAC) Pairing Bigger Buybacks With a Dividend Hike Reveal Its True Capital Priorities?
feed.summary: KLA Corporation (KLAC) recently increased its quarterly dividend to US$2.30 per share and expanded its share repurchase authorization to US$20 billion, signaling strong confidence in its cash generation and a focus on capital returns. These actions, especially the significant buyback authorization, are seen as supporting its investment narrative amidst high earnings multiples and strong stock performance, although the company's valuation remains premium and exposed to policy risks. Despite these financial maneuvers, KLA's core business drivers, which rely on the complexity of chip designs and leading-edge capex, remain unchanged.

feed.title: Salesforce, Inc. Completes $22.5 Billion Senior Notes Offering with New Indenture and Supplemental Terms (March 2026)
feed.summary: Salesforce, Inc. has completed a $22.5 billion senior notes offering with maturities ranging from 2028 to 2066. The net proceeds will be used for accelerated share repurchase agreements, a move intended to return capital to shareholders and potentially boost the company's stock price. This significant debt offering reflects Salesforce's robust capital allocation strategy and management's confidence in its future growth.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines is dropping service to two major US airports
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines will cease service to Washington, D.C.’s Dulles International Airport and Chicago’s O’Hare International Airport starting June 4th. This decision will result in the cancellation of direct flights from Phoenix to these two airports, with customers eligible for refunds. Although Southwest is pulling out of O’Hare and Dulles, it will continue to serve the Chicago area via Midway Airport and the D.C. area through Baltimore’s Washington International and Washington’s Reagan National airports.

feed.title: Snap-on Inc Stock (ISIN: US8335511049) Trades at Potential Discount Amid Institutional Shifts
feed.summary: Snap-on Inc (NYSE: SNA) stock is trading at a potential discount, with its current valuation below intrinsic value based on DCF models, despite recent institutional buying and some insider selling. The company, known for professional tools and diagnostics, shows strong financials with high margins, robust cash flow, and a low beta, making it an attractive prospect for European investors seeking stability and yield in the industrial sector. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus, anticipating modest upside, particularly as the company's defensive traits and franchise model differentiate it from pure cyclical peers.

feed.title: Will W Anthony sells CF Industries (CF) stock worth $10.3 million
feed.summary: Will W Anthony, former President & CEO of CF Industries Holdings, Inc., sold 81,651 shares of CF stock across multiple transactions on March 11 and March 13, 2026, totaling $10.3 million. Following these sales, he directly owns 54,995 shares. This news comes amidst recent strong Q4 results for CF Industries, but also analyst downgrades due to concerns about normalizing fertilizer prices, despite a raised BofA price target and a new low-carbon fertilizer pilot project.

feed.title: Verizon News On Vestberg Move Frontier Deal And Fiber Focus
feed.summary: Hans Vestberg, former CEO of Verizon Communications, has moved to an advisory role at Consello, while Verizon leadership focuses on expanding its fiber reach, bundling broadband and wireless offerings, and pursuing a customer-driven strategy. The company is actively working on the Frontier Communications acquisition and aims to differentiate through service quality and customer experience in a competitive telecom market. Investors are advised to monitor the Frontier deal's structure, Verizon's debt levels, and the impact of its customer-centric approach on financial performance.
